annotations.label: Locally convex topological vector space

anchor.snippet: locally convex topological vector spaces ( LCTVS ) or locally convex spaces are examples of topological vector spaces

annotations.note: The class `LocallyConvexSpace 𝕜 E` asserts that the nhds filter of every point has a basis of convex sets.

annotations.label: Fréchet space

anchor.snippet: Fréchet spaces are locally convex topological vector spaces that are completely metrizable

annotations.note: No `FrechetSpace` class exists; the ingredients (`LocallyConvexSpace`, `IsCompletelyMetrizableSpace`) are present but not bundled.

annotations.label: Locally convex TVS (via convex sets)

anchor.snippet: A topological vector space (TVS) is called locally convex if it has a neighborhood basis

annotations.note: `LocallyConvexSpace` is defined exactly by existence of a convex neighborhood basis at every point.

annotations.label: Neighborhood basis of disks

anchor.snippet: every locally convex TVS has a neighborhood basis of the origin consisting of absolutely convex sets

annotations.note: `nhds_hasBasis_absConvex` shows the 0-neighborhood filter has a basis of absolutely convex (= balanced + convex) sets.

annotations.label: Seminorm

anchor.snippet: A seminorm on

annotations.note: `Seminorm 𝕜 E` is a structure extending `AddGroupSeminorm` with absolute homogeneity in `𝕜`.

annotations.label: Norm (from seminorm via positive definiteness)

anchor.snippet: If [MATH] satisfies positive definiteness

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Norm` / `NormedAddCommGroup` (norm with definiteness) but defines them directly, not as a positive-definite `Seminorm`.

annotations.label: Base of seminorms

anchor.snippet: is called a base of seminorms for

annotations.note: `SeminormFamily` is an indexed family of seminorms; the directedness/finite-sup `base` property is captured by `IsBounded` and `Finset.sup` operations but no `BaseOfSeminorms` predicate is named.

annotations.label: Locally convex space (second version, via seminorms)

anchor.snippet: Definition (second version): A locally convex space is defined to be a vector space

annotations.note: `WithSeminorms p` asserts the topology is the one generated by the seminorm family `p`; `WithSeminorms.toLocallyConvexSpace` connects to the convex-set definition.

annotations.label: Initial topology induced by seminorms

anchor.snippet: induces a canonical vector space topology on

annotations.note: `SeminormFamily.moduleFilterBasis` and `SeminormFamily.topology` build the canonical TVS topology from a seminorm family; `filter_eq_iInf` shows it is the initial topology.

annotations.label: Subbasis at the origin from seminorm preimages

anchor.snippet: The family of preimages

annotations.note: `WithSeminorms.hasBasis_zero_ball` (and `basisSets`) shows the nhds of 0 has a basis of finite intersections of seminorm balls.

annotations.label: Base of continuous seminorms

anchor.section: Bases of seminorms and saturated families
anchor.snippet: is called a base of continuous seminorms if it is a base of seminorms

annotations.note: Continuity of each seminorm in a `WithSeminorms` family is `WithSeminorms.continuous_seminorm`, but no separate `BaseOfContinuousSeminorms` notion is defined.

annotations.label: Saturated family of seminorms

anchor.section: Bases of seminorms and saturated families
anchor.snippet: is called saturated if for any

annotations.note: No `Saturated` predicate for seminorm families is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Neighborhood basis from saturated family

anchor.section: Bases of seminorms and saturated families
anchor.snippet: If [MATH] is a saturated family of continuous seminorms that induces the topology

annotations.note: Statement depends on saturated families which Mathlib does not define.

annotations.label: Fréchet space without continuous norm — equivalence
annotations.note: No equivalent characterization of Fréchet spaces without continuous norm is in Mathlib (Fréchet not even defined).

annotations.label: Net convergence in locally convex space

anchor.snippet: if and only if for all

annotations.note: `WithSeminorms.tendsto_nhds` characterizes convergence of a filter (the filter form of nets) as convergence under each seminorm.

annotations.label: Minkowski functional

anchor.snippet: define the Minkowski functional of

annotations.note: `gauge s x = sInf { r > 0 | x ∈ r • s }` is the Minkowski functional/gauge of a set.

annotations.label: Minkowski functional is a seminorm for balanced convex absorbing sets

anchor.snippet: From this definition it follows that [MATH] is a seminorm if [MATH] is balanced and convex

annotations.note: `gaugeSeminorm hs₀ hs₁ hs₂ : Seminorm 𝕜 E` packages `gauge s` as a seminorm given balanced/convex/absorbent hypotheses.

annotations.label: Auxiliary normed spaces and Banach disks

anchor.section: Ways of defining a locally convex topology
anchor.snippet: Example: auxiliary normed spaces

annotations.note: No `BanachDisk` notion or auxiliary-normed-space construction (the `E_B` subspace) is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Filter base yielding locally convex TVS topology
annotations.note: `LocallyConvexSpace.ofBases` (and `ofBasisZero`) constructs the locally convex topology from a filter base of convex sets.

annotations.label: Separated/total family of seminorms

anchor.snippet: is called total or separated or is said to separate points

annotations.note: Mathlib uses the in-line `∀ x ≠ 0, ∃ i, p i x ≠ 0` separating condition in `WithSeminorms.separating_iff_T1` but does not define a stand-alone `Separating` predicate.

annotations.label: Hausdorff iff separated family of seminorms

anchor.snippet: A locally convex space is Hausdorff if and only if it has a separated family of seminorms

annotations.note: `WithSeminorms.separating_iff_T1` gives the equivalence between separating and T₁ (which, on a TVS, is equivalent to Hausdorff).

annotations.label: Pseudometrizability via countable family of seminorms

anchor.snippet: A locally convex space is pseudometrizable, meaning that its topology arises from a pseudometric, if and only if it has a countable family of seminorms

annotations.note: No theorem of the form `pseudoMetrizable ↔ countable seminorm family` is in Mathlib (general first-countable uniform metrizability exists separately).

annotations.label: Cauchy net in a locally convex space

anchor.snippet: A Cauchy net in a locally convex space is a net

annotations.note: `Cauchy` filter on a uniform space generalizes Cauchy nets; LCS-specific characterization via seminorms is not packaged separately.

annotations.label: Directed family of seminorms

anchor.snippet: One says it is a directed family of seminorms if the family is a directed set

annotations.note: No `Directed` predicate specialised to seminorm families is defined.

annotations.label: Equivalent directed family exists

anchor.snippet: Every family of seminorms has an equivalent directed family

annotations.note: Not formalized; depends on the missing directed-family concept.

annotations.label: Seminormability and normability criteria

anchor.snippet: If the topology of the space is induced from a single seminorm, then the space is seminormable

annotations.note: No `Seminormable`/`Normable` class or characterization theorem is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Seminormable iff bounded neighborhood of origin

anchor.snippet: a locally convex topological vector space is seminormable if and only if the origin has a bounded neighborhood

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IsVonNBounded` and gauge-as-seminorm constructions, but no Kolmogorov normability theorem statement.

annotations.label: Extension property and Hahn-Banach extension property (HBEP)

anchor.snippet: has the extension property if any continuous linear functional

annotations.note: No standalone `ExtensionProperty`/`HBEP` predicate is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach implies HBEP for Hausdorff locally convex

anchor.snippet: The Hahn-Banach theorem guarantees that every Hausdorff locally convex space has the HBEP

annotations.note: Hahn–Banach for LCS (dominated by a continuous seminorm) is `Module.Dual.exists_extension_of_le_seminorm`; the consequence stated in terms of an explicit HBEP predicate is not packaged.

annotations.label: Uncountable-dim space with finest vector topology has HBEP

anchor.snippet: If [MATH] has uncountable dimension and if we endow it with the finest vector topology

annotations.note: Neither the finest vector topology construction nor this example is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kalton: complete metrizable TVS with HBEP is locally convex
annotations.note: Kalton's theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closure characterization in locally convex space

anchor.snippet: if and only if for every

annotations.note: The closure characterization is the general topological `mem_closure_iff_nhds`; in LCS the nhds basis is via seminorm balls (`WithSeminorms.hasBasis_ball`).

annotations.label: Hausdorff LCS embeds in product of Banach spaces

anchor.snippet: Every Hausdorff locally convex space is homeomorphic to a vector subspace of a product of Banach spaces

annotations.note: No such embedding theorem is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Anderson–Kadec theorem

anchor.snippet: The Anderson–Kadec theorem states that every infinite–dimensional separable Fréchet space is homeomorphic to the product space

annotations.note: Anderson–Kadec is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convexity criterion via Minkowski sums

anchor.snippet: A subset [MATH] is convex if and only if

annotations.note: `convex_iff_pointwise_add_subset` characterizes convexity by `a • s + b • s ⊆ s` for `a, b ≥ 0`, `a + b = 1`.

annotations.label: Star-shapedness of convex sets containing origin

anchor.snippet: If [MATH] is a convex set that contains the origin then

annotations.note: `Convex.starConvex` shows any convex set is star-convex at each of its points (in particular at 0 if `0 ∈ s`).

annotations.label: Minkowski sum and scalar multiples of convex sets are convex

anchor.snippet: The Minkowski sum of two convex sets is convex

annotations.note: `Convex.add` and `Convex.smul` (same file) give convexity of sums and scalar multiples.

annotations.label: Open convex subsets via sublinear functionals

anchor.snippet: the open convex subsets of [MATH] are exactly those that are of the form

annotations.note: Mathlib has Hahn–Banach separation lemmas but no explicit `{x | μ x < 1}` characterization of all open convex sets.

annotations.label: Interior and closure of convex set are convex

anchor.snippet: The interior and closure of a convex subset of a TVS is again convex

annotations.note: `Convex.interior` and `Convex.closure` show the interior/closure of a convex set in a TVS is convex.

annotations.label: Interior/closure agreement for convex set with non-empty interior

anchor.snippet: If [MATH] is a convex set with non-empty interior, then the closure of

annotations.note: Mathlib provides both `Convex.closure_interior_eq_closure_of_nonempty_interior` and `Convex.interior_closure_eq_interior_of_nonempty_interior`.

annotations.label: Open line segment from interior to closure lies in interior

anchor.snippet: if [MATH] is a convex subset of a TVS

annotations.note: `Convex.openSegment_interior_closure_subset_interior` (and variants) is exactly this statement.

annotations.label: Separating a vector from a closed subspace by convex neighborhood

anchor.snippet: If [MATH] is a closed vector subspace of a (not necessarily Hausdorff) locally convex space

annotations.note: `exists_open_convex_of_notMem` separates a point from a closed convex set by a convex open neighborhood, of which the closed-subspace case is a corollary.

annotations.label: Closure of convex set invariant under compatible topologies

anchor.snippet: The closure of a convex subset of a locally convex Hausdorff space

annotations.note: No theorem about compatibility of dual pairings and closures of convex sets is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex and disked hull of totally bounded sets are totally bounded

anchor.snippet: the convex hull and the disked hull of a totally bounded set is totally bounded

annotations.note: `totallyBounded_convexHull` covers the convex-hull half; the disked (absolutely convex) hull case is not formalized.

annotations.label: Convex/disked hull of compact set is compact in complete LCS

anchor.snippet: In a complete locally convex space, the convex hull and the disked hull of a compact set are both compact

annotations.note: Only the finite-set case (`Set.Finite.isCompact_convexHull`) is formalized; the compact case in complete LCS is not.

annotations.label: Convex hull of bounded set is bounded in LCS

anchor.snippet: In a locally convex space, convex hulls of bounded sets are bounded

annotations.note: No `IsVonNBounded.convexHull` lemma is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed convex hull of compact set is compact in Fréchet space

anchor.snippet: In a Fréchet space , the closed convex hull of a compact set is compact

annotations.note: Fréchet spaces are not formalized; this Mazur-style result is absent.

annotations.label: Linear combinations of totally bounded sets are totally bounded

anchor.snippet: any linear combination of totally bounded sets is totally bounded

annotations.note: No `TotallyBounded.add` / `TotallyBounded.smul` lemma in this form is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex hull and related hulls

anchor.snippet: the convex hull (respectively, closed convex hull

annotations.note: `convexHull`, `closedConvexHull` (`Mathlib.Analysis.Convex.Topology`), and `absConvexHull` (`Mathlib.Analysis.LocallyConvex.AbsConvex`) cover all the named hulls.

annotations.label: Convex hull of compact set in Hilbert space need not be closed

anchor.snippet: The convex hull of a compact subset of a Hilbert space is not necessarily closed

annotations.note: No counter-example to closedness of `convexHull` of a compact set in a Hilbert space is recorded.

annotations.label: Closed convex hull is precompact in Hausdorff LCS

anchor.snippet: the closed convex hull [MATH] of compact subset [MATH] is not necessarily compact although it is a precompact

annotations.note: `totallyBounded_convexHull` gives totally-bounded (=precompact) for `convexHull`, from which the closure being precompact follows; not stated as a separate lemma.

annotations.label: Closure of convex hull of compact set in quasi-complete LCS

anchor.snippet: In a quasi-complete locally convex TVS, the closure of the convex hull of a compact subset is again compact

annotations.note: Quasi-completeness is not in Mathlib; this Mazur-type result is absent.

annotations.label: Convex hull of precompact set is precompact

anchor.snippet: In a Hausdorff locally convex TVS, the convex hull of a precompact set is again precompact

annotations.note: `totallyBounded_convexHull` is exactly this statement (totally bounded = precompact).

annotations.label: Convex hull of finite union of compact convex sets is compact

anchor.snippet: In any TVS, the convex hull of a finite union of compact convex sets is compact

annotations.note: Only `Set.Finite.isCompact_convexHull` (finite point set) is in Mathlib; the finite union of compact convex sets version is not.

annotations.label: Bipolar theorem

anchor.snippet: The bipolar theorem states that the bipolar

annotations.note: Only `LinearMap.subset_bipolar` (`s ⊆ bipolar s`) is in Mathlib; the full equality with the closed absolutely convex hull is not.

annotations.label: Balanced hull of convex set need not be convex

anchor.snippet: The balanced hull of a convex set is not necessarily convex

annotations.note: No counter-example or remark about non-convexity of `balancedHull` of a convex set is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex combination representation for intersecting convex sets

anchor.snippet: If [MATH] and [MATH] are convex subsets of a topological vector space

annotations.note: No lemma exactly representing points of `convexHull (s ∪ t)` as convex combinations is recorded in this form.

annotations.label: Convex balanced hull

anchor.snippet: the convex balanced hull of [MATH] denoted by [MATH] is the smallest subset

annotations.note: `absConvexHull` is the smallest absolutely convex (balanced + convex) set containing `s`.

annotations.label: Convex balanced hull equals convex hull of balanced hull

anchor.snippet: The convex balanced hull of [MATH] is equal to the convex hull of the balanced hull

annotations.note: `absConvexHull_eq_convexHull_balancedHull` states `absConvexHull 𝕜 s = convexHull 𝕜 (balancedHull 𝕜 s)`.

annotations.label: Closed convex hulls compact implies sum closed convex hull compact

anchor.snippet: If [MATH] are subsets of a TVS [MATH] whose closed convex hulls are compact

annotations.note: No lemma about compactness of the closed convex hull of a sum is in Mathlib (only `IsCompact.add`).

annotations.label: Carathéodory's theorem

anchor.snippet: Carathéodory's theorem

annotations.note: `Caratheodory.convexHull_eq_union` expresses Carathéodory: the convex hull equals the union of convex hulls of affine-independent finite subsets.

annotations.label: Trivial (indiscrete) topology as coarsest locally convex TVS

anchor.snippet: Any vector space [MATH] endowed with the trivial topology

annotations.note: No explicit example/instance giving the indiscrete topology as an LCS is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finest locally convex topology

anchor.snippet: is called the finest locally convex topology on

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the finest locally convex topology on a vector space.

annotations.label: Properties of the finest locally convex topology

anchor.snippet: Every linear map from [MATH] into another locally convex TVS is necessarily continuous

annotations.note: Depends on the missing finest-locally-convex-topology construction.

annotations.label: Normed spaces and Banach spaces as locally convex

anchor.snippet: Every normed space is a Hausdorff locally convex space

annotations.note: `NormedSpace.toLocallyConvexSpace` (instance, ℝ-version) and `NormedSpace.toLocallyConvexSpace'` give the LCS instance for any normed space.

annotations.label: Fréchet spaces as locally convex

anchor.snippet: More generally, every Fréchet space is locally convex

annotations.note: Fréchet spaces are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Space of real-valued sequences as Fréchet space

anchor.snippet: The space [MATH] of real valued sequences with the family of seminorms

annotations.note: The `ℝ^ℕ` Fréchet-space example with coordinate seminorms is not packaged in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weak topology from collection of linear functionals

anchor.snippet: Given any vector space [MATH] and a collection [MATH] of linear functionals on it

annotations.note: `WeakBilin.locallyConvexSpace` makes the weak topology induced by a bilinear pairing a `LocallyConvexSpace`.

annotations.label: Schwartz space

anchor.snippet: Spaces of differentiable functions give other non-normable examples

annotations.note: `SchwartzMap E F` defines the Schwartz space; its locally convex topology by seminorms is also formalized.

annotations.label: Space of smooth compactly supported functions / LF space

anchor.snippet: An important function space in functional analysis is the space

annotations.note: The space `D(U)` of smooth compactly supported functions as an LF-space is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuous functions with topology of uniform convergence on compacts

anchor.snippet: More abstractly, given a topological space

annotations.note: The compact-open topology is in `ContinuousMap.compactOpen`; an explicit `LocallyConvexSpace` instance using suprema-of-seminorm structure is in `Mathlib.Topology.ContinuousMap.LocallyConvex`.

annotations.label: Lp spaces for 0<p<1 lack local convexity

anchor.section: Examples of spaces lacking local convexity
anchor.snippet: The spaces [MATH] for [MATH] are equipped with the F-norm

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `MemLp`/`Lp` for `0 < p ≤ ∞` but does not state that for `0 < p < 1` the space fails to be locally convex.

annotations.label: Space of measurable functions with convergence in measure

anchor.section: Examples of spaces lacking local convexity
anchor.snippet: The space of measurable functions on the unit interval

annotations.note: Topology of convergence in measure as a non-locally-convex TVS is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Continuity criterion for linear maps via seminorms

anchor.snippet: a linear map [MATH] is continuous if and only if for every

annotations.note: `Seminorm.continuous_from_bounded` (and `WithSeminorms.continuous_iff_continuous_comp`) gives the seminorm characterization of continuity.

annotations.label: Continuity of linear operator into locally convex TVS
annotations.note: `WithSeminorms.continuous_iff_continuous_comp` characterizes continuity of a linear map into an LCS by continuity of each composition with a generating seminorm.

annotations.label: Linear functional bounded by seminorm criterion

anchor.snippet: If [MATH] is a real or complex vector space, [MATH] is a linear functional on

annotations.note: Continuity-from-boundedness for linear functionals follows from `Seminorm.continuous_from_bounded` / `continuous_of_isBounded`; a packaged equivalence specifically for functionals is not separate.

annotations.label: Continuity criterion for multilinear maps

anchor.snippet: be a multilinear operator that is linear in each of its

annotations.note: A seminorm continuity criterion for multilinear maps into LCS is not present (only the normed setting via `ContinuousMultilinearMap`).

annotations.label: Existence of continuous norm implies Hausdorff

anchor.snippet: If there exists a continuous norm on a topological vector space [MATH] then [MATH] is necessarily Hausdorff

annotations.note: No theorem of the form `(∃ continuous norm) → T2Space` is stated; the implication is implicit from `WithSeminorms.separating_iff_T1` for a singleton norm.

annotations.label: Continuous linear functional bounded by continuous seminorm

anchor.snippet: is continuous if and only if there exists a continuous seminorm

annotations.note: Closest match is the seminorm continuity criterion applied to `𝕜` with `norm_withSeminorms`; the existential equivalence in this exact phrasing isn't separately packaged.

annotations.label: Discrete topology gives a TVS only on the zero space

anchor.snippet: the discrete topology forms a vector topology on

annotations.note: No such statement (discrete topology TVS ↔ space is `{0}`) is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sequence space ℓ^p with 0<p<1 is not locally convex

anchor.section: Examples of spaces lacking local convexity
anchor.snippet: The sequence space

annotations.note: Failure of local convexity for `ℓ^p`, `0<p<1`, is not formalized.

annotations.label: Non-locally-convex examples have trivial dual

anchor.section: Examples of spaces lacking local convexity
anchor.snippet: Both examples have the property that any continuous linear map to the real numbers is

annotations.note: Triviality of the topological dual for these specific examples is not in Mathlib.
